hypothesis_en: Mr President, on 11 March all MEPs joined Parliament, horrified by the news of the terrorist massacre in Madrid at 7.30 a.m. Let me first express my condolences and solidarity with the victims and their relatives, as well as my rejection of all forms of terrorism. I have given in to so much blood and so much pain that its authorship could have been the work of some ultra-radical Basque ETA fanatics. By then, the early call to the Spanish police of the witness who saw three hooded in Alcalá de Henares and had found the van with the detonators and the quote with the verses of the Koran and also the statements against the attack and an authorship of ETA by the spokesman of Batasuna had already occurred. And yet the authorship of the resolution by the European Parliament and the United Nations Security Council was artificially pressed and maintained, and I understood that the Spanish government of Mr Aznar's People's Party was trying to divert its responsibility for participating in the invasion of Iraq against the opinion of the Spanish people, the UN and international law, and was taking advantage once again. The discovery of a backpack with explosives and a mobile phone manipulated by Islamists, nor the repeated statements claiming the attacks by Al Qaeda, did not help them. And what is worse, millions of European citizens and governments in other states were brazenly deceived by the popular government, which did not care about the risk of repeated attacks and deaths in other cities, so that the Spaniards did not know until after the elections on Sunday 14 that the horror suffered in Madrid was a response to the unjust and illegal war. Fortunately, the Spanish people were able to claim the truth and expel from the government those who tried to deceive them, and I hope that we will soon realize that the best recipe against terrorism is to end the injustice and poverty that are so widespread in the world and so we can live in peace.
reference_en: President (1.7) (1.82) as of the eleventh of March the Members of the Parliament met hearing that (1.2) tragic events had happened in Madrid at seven thirty in the morning I would like to express my solidarity with the victims as well as my outright condemnation of terrorism that morning (1.35) I had heard with particular emotion the fact that it was possible that it was some fanatics from the ultra-right from ETA that could have been responsible the other day I heard a Belgian television chain then saying that it was likely to be Islamic terrorism at midday it was mentioned that the eleventh of March could be sugges= could be the European day of solidarity with victims of ~terrororism at that point in time there was confusion in Spain about who was responsible the van with verses of the Quran in it had been found and (1.19) everybody was unclear about just who was responsibility who was responsible Herri Batasuna had already denied responsibility and yet in spite of that artificially ETA was mentioned in the Parliament's resolution as well as in the resolution of the Security Council of the United Nations this made me understand that the Spanish government under Mr Aznar was attempting to in fact (1.19) slough off its responsibilities in involving Spain in the war in Iraq and in illegal acts internationally and was therefore blaming ETA and nationalism for the problems that had been caused by the intolerance and arrogance of s= the Spanish government's attitude (1.36) when timers and a mobile phone were found and when Al-Qaeda claimed responsibility the PP continued to blame ETA through Aznar through the ambassadors of Spain as well as the Minister of the Interior Acebes and millions of citizens of other European countries were in fact conned by the Spanish government who continued to say that it was ETA that was responsible for these events and it was only after the elections in Spain that it became clear that in fact it it was as a result of the illegal actions in Iraq of the Spanish government that the events had taken place let's hope that we start to realise that the best way of beating terrorism is ensuring justice for all and the eradication of poverty which will= is the only path to peace thank you
source_es: señor Presidente el pasado once de marzo todos los eurodiputados nos incorporamos al Parlamento horrorizados por la noticia de la masacre terrorista en Madrid a las siete y media de la mañana permítanme en primer lugar expresar mi pésame condolencia y solidaridad para= con las víctimas y sus allegados así como mi rechazo a toda clase de terrorismo aquella mañana yo había sentido como un mazazo especial añadido a tanta sangre y a tanto dolor que su autoría pudiera haber sido obra de unos fanáticos ultrarradicales vascos de ETA (1.02) a media mañana tuve la oportunidad de escuchar una cadena de televisión belga que apuntaba a la posibilidad de su autoría por un terrorismo islámico cuando a mediodía en el hemiciclo se planteó como enmienda oral la declaración del once de marzo como día europeo contra el terrorismo el diputado proponente culpó a ETA para entonces ya se había producido la llamada temprana a la policía española del testigo que vio a tres encapuchados en Alcalá de Henares y se había encontrado la furgoneta con los detonadores y la cita con los versos del Corán y también se habían producido las declaraciones contrarias al atentado y de una autoría de ETA por parte del portavoz de Batasuna ya se sabía que el atentado no había sido obra de ETA y pese a ello se presionó y se logró mantener artificialmente la autoría de ETA en la resolución del Parlamento Europeo y en la del Consejo de Seguridad de Naciones Unidas (1.09) por todo ello comprendí que el Gobierno español del Partido Popular del Señor Aznar estaba tratando de desviar su responsabilidad por participar en la invasión de Iraq en contra de la opinión de los ciudadanos españoles de la ONU y de la legalidad internacional y aprovechaba una vez más para echar la culpa a ETA y a los nacionalismos de los problemas que ~autoristarismos e intolerancias como la suya han generado en España y en el mundo tampoco le sirvió de nada el descubrimiento de una mochila con detonadores explosivos y un teléfono móvil manipulado por islamistas ni los reiterados comunicados reivindicando los atentados por la organización Al Qaeda el Partido Popular seguía echando la culpa a ETA mediante llamadas personales del Señor Aznar a los principales diarios y de la ministra de Exteriores a los embajadores así como a través de las inefables comparecencias públicas del ministro Acebes y lo que es peor millones de ciudadanos europeos y gobiernos de otros Estados fueron descaradamente engañados por el Gobierno popular a quien no importó un bledo el riesgo de repetición de atentados y muertes en otras ciudades con tal de que los españoles no supieran hasta después de las elecciones del domingo catorce que el horror padecido en Madrid era una respuesta a la guerra injusta e ilegal de Iraq afortunadamente la ciudadanía española supo reivindicar la verdad y despachar del Gobierno a quienes le pretendieron engañar y ojalá que nos convenzamos pronto de que la mejor ~rejceta contra el terrorismo es acabar con la injusticia y la pobreza que tan extendidas están por el mundo y así podremos vivir en paz muchas gracias señor Presidente
